Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Go on Embedded Systems and WebAssembly 171 upvotes, 23 comments
  2. Herbie: Automatically improve imprecise floating point formulas 146 upvotes, 21 comments
  3. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 174 upvotes, 30 comments
  4. iNaturalist 457 upvotes, 109 comments
  5. Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs 722 upvotes, 186 comments
  6. Apple: Embarrassingly Simple Self-Distillation Improves Code Generation 142 upvotes, 31 comments
  7. What changes when you turn a Linux box into a router 186 upvotes, 46 comments
  8. Fake Fans 124 upvotes, 30 comments
  9. The FAA’s flight restriction for drones is an attempt to criminalize filming ICE 435 upvotes, 138 comments
  10. Artemis II crew take “spectacular” image of Earth 833 upvotes, 287 comments
  11. Some Unusual Trees 72 upvotes, 18 comments
  12. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 336 upvotes, 125 comments
  13. Run Linux containers on Android, no root required 153 upvotes, 56 comments
  14. The most-disliked people in the publishing industry 17 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. OpenClaw privilege escalation vulnerability 423 upvotes, 207 comments
  16. How to make a sliding, self-locking, and predator-proof chicken coop door (2020) 112 upvotes, 47 comments
  17. The house is a work of art: Frank Lloyd Wright 93 upvotes, 40 comments
  18. The CMS is dead. Long live the CMS 20 upvotes, 5 comments
  19. The Cathedral, the Bazaar, and the Winchester Mystery House 14 upvotes, 3 comments
  20. Delve removed from Y Combinator 370 upvotes, 227 comments
  21. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 809 upvotes, 633 comments
  22. Claude Code Found a Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years 89 upvotes, 58 comments
  23. Sequential Optimal Packing for PCB Placement 13 upvotes, 5 comments
  24. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 121 upvotes, 96 comments
  25. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 100 upvotes, 112 comments
  26. Improving my focus by giving up my big monitor 114 upvotes, 134 comments
  27. Why are we still using Markdown? 150 upvotes, 215 comments
  28. Jack Dorsey says Block employees now bring prototypes, not slides, to meetings 5 upvotes, 2 comments
  29. F-15E jet shot down over Iran 516 upvotes, 1156 comments
  30. Mbodi AI (YC P25) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 9:00pm, 4 April 2026. Source here.
